line1.description: Two-line description of the portion of a mark (text words or design features) for which the applicant disclaims exclusive rights. Used on MARK_mark1a (Mark Disclaimer); the disclaimer form is filed alongside a registration to carve out elements (e.g., generic terms or descriptive features) from the exclusive scope while still allowing registration of the broader mark.

line2.description: Two-line description of the portion of a mark (text words or design features) for which the applicant disclaims exclusive rights. Used on MARK_mark1a (Mark Disclaimer); the disclaimer form is filed alongside a registration to carve out elements (e.g., generic terms or descriptive features) from the exclusive scope while still allowing registration of the broader mark.

type.description: Mutually-exclusive enum classifying the mark: 'trademark' | 'service_mark' | 'combined_service_trademark' | 'certification_mark' | 'collective_mark'. Distinct from mark.type_and_class_number on MARK_mark1, which combines type and class number into a single free-text field. mark.type is the structured form of the type-only concept and appears on forms (like MARK_mark1a) that decompose the categories into checkboxes. Rubric for the mark family should treat mark.type as derivable from mark.type_and_class_number when only the latter is set, and vice-versa.
